title: Local tea outing: Bewdley / Trimpley with Margaret & Helen

main_route.excerpt: We turned off the main road at Bewdley + drove along a very narrow lane to sit in a field with nice wooden shacks round the edge of it. The train went by a few times.
Then we took Margaret + Helen back to Trimpley + went home.
Then we took Margaret + Helen back to Trimpley + went home.

boundary_flags: mixed_start: page B_0162_L contains an earlier diary entry (Oswestry visit) before the May 17th tea outing entry; only the tea outing from 'May 17th Sunday' onward belongs to this trip
summary: A short local Sunday afternoon outing from Birmingham, taking family friends Margaret and Helen out for tea. The party drove to a field near Bewdley (alongside the Severn Valley Railway), then dropped Margaret and Helen at Trimpley before returning home.
extraction_notes: The page date metadata records '1998-05-17' but May 17th was a Sunday in 2009, not 1998, confirming the trip_id year. The page has mixed_start=true: the upper portion describes a separate Oswestry trip; the tea outing begins at the 'May 17th Sunday' heading. The field visited near Bewdley with wooden shacks and a passing train is consistent with a location alongside the Severn Valley Railway between Bewdley and Trimpley Reservoir. No ferries, crossings, or overnight stays; entirely within GB.
